prompt: A CivicSort Operations platform capable of running fully offline supports city sanitation teams in standardizing waste-sorting guidance, inspections, and performance oversight from a single web application. Field Inspectors use a Yew-based browser interface on laptops or tablets to search the Waste-Sorting Knowledge Base by item name, alias, or common misspelling, with fuzzy-match results ranked by configurable weights and clearly showing the applicable region and rule version, disposal requirements, and attached reference images. Inspectors then complete scheduled check-ins using reusable task templates that can be grouped (for example, “Weekly HOA Dumpster Area” with subtasks for signage, contamination, and bin condition), each task having a defined cycle, a time window such as 8:00 AM–6:00 PM, fault tolerance such as allowing 1 missed occurrence per 30 days, and make-up rules such as completing within 48 hours to avoid marking overdue; the UI provides immediate validation feedback and an in-app reminder inbox. Reviewers work in a dedicated review workspace to score submitted inspections or disputed classifications via configurable scorecards with weighted dimensions, required comment fields, rating levels, and consistency checks that flag contradictory ratings before submission; assignments can be automatic or manual, support blind review, and enforce conflict-of-interest recusals based on the reviewer’s department or previous involvement. Operations Admins and Department Managers use an admin console to view user/item/work-order overviews, KPI dashboards (conversion to correct sorting guidance, reuse rate of templates, and 30/60/90-day retention), configure education campaigns and promos (for example, “Spring Cleanup Week 04/15/2026–04/22/2026”), manage categories/tags, and export audit-ready reports. The backend uses Actix-web to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Yew frontend, with PostgreSQL as the system of record for users, roles, device bindings, knowledge-base versions, tasks, submissions, reviews, notifications, and analytics aggregates. All data and files are stored locally; images are validated by type and size (JPEG/PNG only, up to 5 MB) and fingerprinted to prevent duplicate uploads. The Messaging & Notification Center is implemented as an offline template engine with variables and trigger rules (booking/reschedule/refund-style events mapped to inspection scheduling, task reschedules, appeal outcomes, and itinerary reminders); it generates in-app messages immediately and produces queued SMS/email/push payload files for later manual transfer, while still tracking delivery status, failures, and retry attempts within the local environment. Risk control includes rate limiting (for example, 60 requests/minute/user), anti-bot throttling on high-frequency actions, anomalous login detection, and device-account binding with step-up verification for critical actions (re-enter password for exports, rule rollbacks, or result publication). Authentication is local username/password only, passwords must be at least 12 characters with Argon2 hashing, accounts lock for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts, sessions idle-timeout after 30 minutes, and sensitive fields are masked in the UI and encrypted at rest using application-managed keys; all permissioned actions are recorded to an immutable audit log and exportable to CSV/PDF without requiring any network connectivity.
